sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Does there exist a profinite group$G$that is not free but can be represented as a~projective limit$G= _{ }(G/N_{ })$, where all the$G/N_{ }$are free profinite groups of finite ranks? The finiteness condition on the ranks of the$G/N_{ }$is essential. Such a group$G$cannot satisfy the first axiom of countability (O.\,V.\,Mel'nikov, Dokl. AN BSSR, { 24}, no.\,11 (1980), 968--970 (Russian)).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
